description: The Finance department has flagged anomalies in Q4 expense data. An expense management system is running at http://localhost:8080 with a consolidated ledger of 50 transactions from 8 employees. An investigation brief with full details is on the Desktop. Investigate three fraud patterns: (1) Shell Company Invoices — identify all submissions to vendors named 'Pacific Coast Consulting' and 'Sterling Associates', and verify these vendors do not appear in the approved vendor directory on the Vendors page; (2) Double-Claim Detection — find cases where two different employees submitted expenses with matching vendor, date, and amount, indicating the same event was reimbursed twice; (3) Budget Code Misuse — cross-reference each transaction's project code (visible in the transaction detail view) against the Project Directory page to flag expenses charged to non-existent project codes or to codes whose project description clearly does not match the expense category. Compile all findings into ~/Documents/Fraud_Investigation/report.txt organized by pattern with three labeled sections. For each flagged transaction include: employee name, transaction ID, date, vendor, amount, and which pattern it matches. Bookmark the Expense Ledger page and the Project Directory page in a Chrome bookmark folder named 'Active Investigation'. Set Chrome download directory to ~/Documents/Fraud_Investigation.

mismatched_project_entries.TXN-027: Client dinner charged to PRJ-003 (Server Infrastructure Upgrade)
mismatched_project_entries.TXN-044: Cloud hosting charged to PRJ-007 (Office Renovation Phase 2)
